moat.description: Extreme switching costs; once a SMB has QuickBooks, they almost never leave.
moat.analysisSummary: Intuit owns the **Financial Workflow** for small businesses and individuals:

analysisPoints.title: Mission-Critical Software
analysisPoints.text: QuickBooks is the system of record for millions. Migrating accounting data is a nightmare, creating a "sticky" moat.

analysisPoints.title: Regulatory Tailwinds
analysisPoints.text: Tax complexity ensures TurboTax remains a necessity for the consumer segment.

analysisPoints.title: Data Advantage
analysisPoints.text: Intuit sees more SMB transaction data than almost any bank, allowing for superior credit underwriting.

growth.description: Credit Karma integration and AI-assisted tax filing driving ARPU.

valuation.description: Trading between bear ($300) and base ($480) — compressed multiple reflects IRS Direct File overhang and tariff-driven SMB uncertainty; priced attractively for a business with 7M+ switching-cost moats.

bear.description: IRS Direct File gains significant traction, SMB churn rises in a prolonged macro slowdown, and AI alternatives erode TurboTax's value proposition.
points: TurboTax unit count declines 10-15% as IRS free-filing expands to all states
SMB failures in tariff-sensitive sectors accelerate QuickBooks churn
Credit Karma revenue stalls as consumer credit conditions remain tight

base.description: QuickBooks pricing power and Intuit Assist AI monetization offset TurboTax headwinds, with steady 10-12% platform revenue growth.
points: Mid-market QuickBooks expansion drives ARPU above $2,000/customer
Intuit Assist AI copilot achieves meaningful paid penetration in SMB segment
Operating margins expand 100-200 bps as AI reduces support cost structure

bull.description: QuickBooks becomes the financial operating system for 15M+ SMBs globally, with B2B payments and lending creating a financial super-app.
points: International QuickBooks expansion reaches critical mass in UK, Canada, and ANZ
B2B payments volume through QuickBooks doubles, adding a high-margin revenue layer
Credit Karma's lending platform inflects as rate cycle normalizes

learnedInterfaces.note: TurboTax's guided interview format is being challenged by AI tax assistants that offer conversational, agent-driven filing.

businessLogic.note: AI can increasingly replicate tax preparation and small business accounting logic; IRS Direct File is a zero-cost competitor.

publicDataAccess.note: Tax forms and IRS regulations are public — AI can navigate them, reducing Intuit's simplification value proposition.

talentScarcity.note: AI has democratized access to tax and accounting expertise, reducing the barrier that made TurboTax's guided logic so valuable.

bundling.note: TurboTax + QuickBooks + Credit Karma + Mailchimp = a unique SMB financial platform covering taxes, accounting, credit, and marketing.

proprietaryData.note: Tax return data, financial records, and credit data for 100M+ users = an irreplaceable financial profile dataset.

regulatoryLockIn.note: IRS authorized e-file partner status, state tax agency relationships, payroll tax compliance, and financial institution integrations.

networkEffects.note: CPA-client ecosystem: accountants recommend TurboTax → clients use it → data improves. QuickBooks SMB network self-reinforces.

transactionEmbedding.note: Payroll runs, invoicing, tax filing, expense tracking, and lending all embedded in the daily operations of 7M+ small businesses.

systemOfRecord.note: QuickBooks is the system of record for 7M+ small business finances; TurboTax is the tax history record for tens of millions of households.

tenMoats.verdict: Intuit's proprietary tax data and SMB system-of-record position are genuine AI-resilient moats. The IRS Direct File threat is real but limited to simple returns — Intuit's platform breadth compounds its defense.
